filled.diagnosis_and_prognosis: The claimant sustained multiple significant injuries as a result of the index incident, including an open (compound) fracture of the right tibia and fibula, a right femoral fracture treated with intramedullary nail fixation, a right wrist fracture treated with open reduction and internal fixation with two plates and multiple screws, right knee ACL and MCL ligament injuries with suspected patellar involvement, vertebral fractures (managed conservatively), a splenic laceration (managed conservatively), and compartment syndrome of the right lower leg requiring fasciotomy and subsequent skin grafting. The claimant also developed early sepsis approximately five months following application of the Ilizarov frame, and nerve conduction studies and EMG have confirmed substantial nerve damage to the right lower leg. At approximately four years post-incident, the claimant continues to experience moderate symptoms referable to the right tibia and fibula with ongoing aching and ankle pain, moderate symptoms referable to the right femur with hip pain and intermittent cracking, severe symptoms referable to the right wrist with significantly reduced range of motion and grip strength, restricted right knee flexion to approximately 95 degrees, chronic foot cramping related to nerve damage, and ongoing insomnia and low mood for which he is prescribed Mirtazapine 30mg. The prognosis is that the claimant's residual symptoms, including chronic pain, reduced mobility, and restricted wrist function, are likely to be permanent and represent the claimant's long-term condition. Some marginal improvement may occur with continued physiotherapy and exercise, but a full recovery is not anticipated.
filled.effect_on_life: The claimant reports that his injuries have had a profound impact upon his daily life and leisure activities. He was required to sleep downstairs for approximately the first five months following the incident owing to his inability to manage the stairs. He required assistance from his partner with bathing and personal care during this period. The claimant is no longer able to ride a bicycle, despite having attempted recumbent bicycles, as his right leg causes significant pain upon pedalling. He is unable to play football. He is unable to go on bike rides with his daughter, which he reports as being particularly distressing. He had to cancel a music festival that had been booked for the summer following the incident. The claimant reports feeling self-conscious about the appearance of his right leg and avoids wearing shorts in public. He reports chronic insomnia since the accident and states he has not had a good night's sleep since the incident. He also reports that prolonged sitting causes his leg to seize up, necessitating frequent movement.
filled.effect_on_work: The claimant was employed as a roofer and was running his own small roofing business with a team of workers at the time of the incident. He has been unable to return to his previous occupation since the injury. He closed his business as a direct consequence of his injuries. He reports that he is unable to swing a hammer with his right hand owing to severe wrist pain and reduced grip strength, and he does not feel safe climbing ladders due to the risk of falling and re-injuring his compromised right leg and wrist. He has undertaken a couple of minor odd jobs but has not returned to construction work. He is currently in receipt of Universal Credit. The loss of his business and livelihood has contributed to his symptoms of depression.
examination_findings: The claimant's right lower leg demonstrated multiple well-healed scars consistent with the reported open fracture, fasciotomy, skin grafting, and Ilizarov frame pin sites.
One pin site on the right lower leg was noted to be tethered to the underlying muscle.
Right knee flexion was restricted to approximately 95 degrees, compared with full flexion on the contralateral side.
Right wrist dorsiflexion was reduced to approximately 30 per cent of normal.
Right wrist palmarflexion was reduced to approximately 50 per cent of normal.
Right hand grip strength was subjectively reduced.
A surgical scar was visible on the dorsum of the right wrist consistent with the reported open reduction and internal fixation.
A further surgical scar was visible on the volar aspect of the right wrist.
A scar was noted running down the right knee consistent with surgical intervention.
A palpable lump was noted in the medial arch of the right foot, reported by the claimant to be related to nerve damage from the Ilizarov frame.
filled.immediate_medical_attendance: The claimant was attended to at the scene by an off-duty paramedic who happened to be passing and who administered ketamine for pain relief. An ambulance arrived approximately 45 minutes later. The claimant was transferred to the Northern General Hospital, Sheffield, where he was assessed in the Accident and Emergency department. A CT scan was performed which identified an open (compound) fracture of the right tibia and fibula, a right femoral fracture, a right wrist fracture, vertebral fractures, and a splenic laceration. The claimant was admitted and underwent emergency surgery, during which an external fixation device was applied to the right lower leg, the open wound was cleaned, and a fasciotomy was performed for suspected compartment syndrome.
filled.incident_description: The claimant reports that on 11/06/2022, he was crossing a road at Tollbar where there was a central island. He was struck by a motor vehicle before he was able to reach the island. The claimant states that the vehicle was allegedly speeding and that the driver failed to stop at the scene, making this a hit-and-run incident. The claimant was knocked a considerable distance from the point of impact. He lost consciousness following the collision.
filled.living_arrangements: The claimant was required to sleep downstairs for approximately the first five months following the incident. He had access to a downstairs toilet but required assistance from his partner to get upstairs for bathing. He needed help getting in and out of the bath owing to his right wrist fracture preventing him from supporting his own body weight.
filled.medical_history: The claimant reports no significant past medical history prior to the index incident. He describes himself as having been fit and healthy before the accident. He is currently prescribed Mirtazapine 30mg for depression and insomnia, which he has been taking for approximately two months at the time of the consultation.
filled.past_accidents: The claimant reports no previous accidents or injuries.
filled.photo_id_confirmation: The claimant was unable to produce photographic identification at the time of the consultation. He stated that he holds a driving licence but was unable to locate it during the appointment.
physical_symptoms: The claimant reports moderate ongoing aching in the right tibia and fibula region, with associated right ankle pain at the site of former Ilizarov frame pin insertion.

The claimant reports moderate aching in the right hip, which he attributes to the intramedullary nail fixation of the right femur; the hip intermittently cracks upon standing and is accompanied by acute pain requiring a period of rest before mobilisation.

The claimant reports severe pain in the right wrist, localised to the dorsum of the hand and over the distal radius and ulna, with significantly reduced dorsiflexion (approximately 30 per cent of normal), reduced palmarflexion (approximately 50 per cent of normal), and diminished grip strength.
The claimant reports restricted right knee flexion to approximately 95 degrees with associated tightness and pain, preventing activities such as cycling.
The claimant reports chronic cramping in the right foot, particularly in the region of the medial arch, which he relates to confirmed nerve damage in the right lower leg.
The claimant reports a tethered pin site scar on the right lower leg which causes intermittent discomfort.
The claimant reports mild to moderate residual back pain referable to the vertebral fractures sustained in the incident.
The claimant reports that his right leg stiffens up with prolonged sitting, requiring him to move frequently.
The claimant reports general weakness and weight loss following the incident due to prolonged immobility and medication side effects.
filled.pre_existing_conditions: The claimant reports no pre-existing medical conditions. He describes himself as having been fit and healthy prior to the index incident.
filled.psychological_symptoms: The claimant reports symptoms of depression which he attributes to the significant impact of his injuries upon his daily life, his inability to work, the loss of his business, and his reduced capacity to engage in activities with his daughter and social activities. He reports feeling self-conscious about the appearance of his right leg and avoids wearing shorts in public, indicating a degree of body image disturbance. He reports chronic insomnia since the accident, stating that he has not had a good night's sleep since the incident. During the period with the Ilizarov frame, the claimant's sleep pattern became significantly disrupted due to medication and prolonged immobility. He is currently prescribed Mirtazapine 30mg, which he has been taking for approximately two months, and is under the care of a psychologist. He also reports feeling paranoid about potential re-injury to his leg.
filled.rehabilitative_treatment: The claimant underwent 15 sessions of physiotherapy addressing his right leg, right wrist, and general rehabilitation following prolonged immobility. He was also advised to continue with exercises to maintain muscle strength and joint mobility. He is currently under the care of a psychologist and has been prescribed Mirtazapine 30mg for depression and insomnia.
filled.subsequent_medical_attendance: Following initial emergency surgery, the claimant's external fixation device was converted to an Ilizarov frame approximately three weeks post-injury, which remained in situ for approximately eleven months. He underwent intramedullary nail fixation of the right femoral fracture. He underwent open reduction and internal fixation of the right wrist fracture with two plates and approximately eleven screws, under nerve block anaesthesia. Approximately five months after the Ilizarov frame was fitted, the claimant developed early sepsis secondary to infection at the frame pin sites, for which he attended the Northern General Hospital, Sheffield, and received treatment including antibiotics. He subsequently underwent skin grafting to the fasciotomy site on the right lower leg. He later required surgical removal of a calcified bone fragment from his right leg. He underwent nerve conduction studies and electromyography which confirmed substantial nerve damage to the right lower leg. He received 15 sessions of physiotherapy for his right leg, right wrist, and general rehabilitation. He is currently prescribed Mirtazapine 30mg and is under the care of a psychologist.
